title: Austin Robotaxi Fleet Hits 10 Unsupervised Model Ys
summary: Tesla's Austin unsupervised Robotaxi fleet reached 10 Model Y vehicles, the highest confirmed count to date. This reflects ongoing ramp-up in the primary Texas hub.
impact_reason: Demonstrates continued fleet growth in Austin to a record 10 vehicles, supporting steady unsupervised scaling velocity in the core operational area.

title: Dallas and Houston Robotaxi Fleets Reach 2 Vehicles Each After Launch
summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data shows Tesla deployed 2 unsupervised vehicles in both Dallas and Houston by April 20, following initial single-vehicle launches. Austin fleet stands at 13 active unsupervised vehicles.
impact_reason: Confirms small but growing unsupervised deployments in new Texas cities, indicating initial scaling momentum beyond Austin without major hurdles.

title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla launched unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston on April 18, with initial geofence areas defined and video evidence showing no safety driver. This triples Texas operations alongside ongoing Austin scaling.
impact_reason: Expands unsupervised operations to two additional Texas cities, directly increasing the state's robotaxi footprint and supporting faster overall fleet scaling despite small initial deployments.

title: Tesla Discloses 14 Crashes in Austin Robotaxi Operations
summary: In a February filing, Tesla reported 14 crashes involving its Austin robotaxis since launch.
impact_reason: Disclosed crashes introduce mild safety scrutiny that could slightly slow regulatory approvals or public acceptance, indirectly dampening unsupervised fleet scaling velocity in Austin.
